units.title: Top-level login procedure and explicit/implicit split
units.reason: The login section defines a general Login procedure, states it may be explicit or implicit, and identifies explicit login as using FLOGI or PLOGI ELS exchanges carrying Service Parameters and LS_ACC payload responses.

evidence_refs.quote: The Login procedure is a method by which an Nx_Port establishes its operating environment with a
Login between an Nx_Port and the Fabric or between two Nx_Ports may use an explicit or implicit
Explicit Login is accomplished using a Login (FLOGI or PLOGI) ELS
gin Exchange.  The LS_ACC contains the Service Parameters of the Responder

units.title: Fabric-oriented login procedure using FLOGI
units.reason: The document defines explicit Fabric Login, its request composition, destination addressing, S_ID assignment options, response branches, relogin behavior, class/SOF constraints, and completion rules for originator and responder.

evidence_refs.quote: The explicit Fabric Login procedure shall require an Nx_Port to transmit a Fabric Login (FLOGI) Link
The Nx_Port shall transmit the FLOGI in a new Exchange.
set the D_ID to the well-known F_Port address (i.e., FFFFFEh).
6.2.2.3  Responses to Explicit Fabric Login
6.2.2.5  Relogin with the Fabric
Fabric Login completion - Originator
Fabric Login completion - Responder

units.title: Fabric Login response branch set
units.reason: The Fabric Login procedure includes explicit branch handling for LS_ACC from a Fabric, LS_ACC from another Nx_Port in point-to-point, F_BSY, P_BSY, F_RJT, P_RJT, LS_RJT, no response, and self-connection cases, each with specified next actions.

evidence_refs.quote: The following are possible responses the Nx_Port may receive when transmitting a FLOGI:
a) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI
b) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI
F_BSY with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
d) P_BSY Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
e) F_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
P_RJT Sequence, with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
g) LS_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
h) No Response may indicate a delivery error
If the received N_Port_Name is equal to its N_Port_Name

units.title: N_Port Login response branches and collision handling
units.reason: The N_Port Login procedure defines response handling for fabric-present and no-fabric cases, including normal LS_ACC, F_BSY/P_BSY, F_RJT/P_RJT, LS_RJT, no response, self-connection, and incoming-PLOGI collision resolution based on N_Port_Name ordering.

evidence_refs.quote: 6.3.2.3  Responses to N_Port Login - Fabric present
a) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI
b) F_BSY with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
F_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
d) P_BSY Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
e) P_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
PLOGI Sequence.  The D_ID is the N_Port_ID of receiving Nx_Port.
sends LS_RJT to the originating Nx_Port with reason code "Command already in progress".
6.3.2.5  Responses to N_Port Login - No Fabric present
e) PLOGI Sequence.  The D_ID is the address identifier of receiving Nx_Port.

units.title: Process-level login procedure using PRLI
units.reason: The Process Login section defines PRLI as an ELS request establishing operating environment between related process groups, distinguishes implicit and explicit forms, places PRLI after successful N_Port Login, and allows establishment or modification of image pairs and FC-4 service parameters.

evidence_refs.quote: The Process Login (PRLI) ELS request shall be used to establish the operating environment between
A Process Login remains in effect until a process logout occurs.
There are 2 types of Process Login:
b) Explicit Process Login is accomplished by using the PRLI ELS Sequence within a separate Ex-
PRLI, if required, is performed after N_Port Login is successful and prior to other FC-4 transfers.
An image pair may be established or modified with a PRLI request and LS_ACC reply Sequence set.

units.title: Image and image-pair structure used by process login
units.reason: The PRLI text defines an image as a group of related processes identified by Process_Associator, and defines the image pair identity as the combination of D_ID, Responder Process_Associator, S_ID, and Originator Process_Associator, with explicit references to figures 4 and 5.

evidence_refs.quote: A group of related processes is known as an image and is identified by the Process_Associator (see
figure 4).  The combination of the D_ID, Responder Process_Associator, S_ID and Originator
Process_Associator identify the image pair (see figure 5).

units.title: PRLI establishment, gating, and retry/error handling
units.reason: The PRLI procedure states when an image pair is considered established, requires prior N_Port Login, defines behavior when link errors or not-logged-in conditions occur, and allows retry for LS_RJT/P_BSY/F_BSY/P_RJT/F_RJT or no valid response.

evidence_refs.quote: The Nx_Port originating the PRLI request shall not consider the image pair to be established until it
If a link error is detected when a PRLI request is received
and the image pair is not established.  If the recipient Nx_Port is not logged in with the requesting
port it shall reply with a LOGO ELS Sequence, or with an LS_RJT ELS Sequence
existing image pair is unaffected.  If an LS_RJT, P_BSY, F_BSY, P_RJT, or F_RJT response is re-
a PRLI request receives no valid response, the Nx_Port should retry the request.

units.title: PRLI PA-relationship variants and operating modes
units.reason: The PRLI section defines multiple Process_Associator relationship variants and distinguishes informative versus binding mode, where binding is controlled by the Establish Image Pair bit and requires more precise knowledge of responder PAs/capabilities.

evidence_refs.quote: Process_Associator (PA) images may exist in the following relationships.
7.1.2.2  PA not supported
7.1.2.3  PA required by originator, supported by responder
7.1.2.4  PA required by responder, supported by originator
7.1.2.5  PA required by originator and responder
7.1.3.1  Informative mode
7.1.3.2  Binding mode
Binding or Informative mode is determined by the setting of the Establish Image Pair bit in the PRLI

units.title: PRLI page validity-bit protocol rules
units.reason: The protocol subsection defines request/response page composition rules for PRLI based on PA requirements, including which Originator/Responder PA validity bits are set and whether the page identifies binding operations against one image or an entire Nx_Port side.

evidence_refs.quote: 7.1.4.1  PA required by originator and responder
For each PRLI request page the Originator and Responder PA validity bits are set to one and valid
For each LS_ACC response page, the Originator and Responder PA validity bits are set to one and
7.1.4.2  PA required by originator, supported by responder
For each PRLI request page the Originator PA validity bit is set to one and the Responder PA validity
7.1.4.3  PA supported by originator, required by responder
For each PRLI request page, the Originator PA validity bit is set to zero and the Responder PA valid-
Each page identifies a Binding mode PRLI operation between the Originator Nx_Port and one image

units.title: Process-level logout using PRLO
units.reason: The Process Logout section defines PRLO as invalidating the operating environment created by prior PRLI, freeing associated resources, allowing type-specific resets or image-pair removal, defining acceptance/retry behavior, and requiring ULP-managed orderly termination plus ABTS for active exchanges.

evidence_refs.quote: The Process Logout (PRLO) ELS request shall be used to request invalidation of the operating envi-
frees resources committed by a previous PRLI function.
